title: Task Contract
description: Binding contract created before any non-trivial edit. Captures goal, must/must-not/non-goals, acceptance criteria, allowed scope, preserved invariants, blocking unknowns, and risk tier. Prevents early requirements from being silently dropped.

task_id.description: Stable identifier for this task (e.g., slug or UUID).

base_commit.description: Git commit SHA at which the task starts. Used by the verifier to create a clean checkout.

goal.description: One-sentence statement of what the task must achieve.

must.description: Hard requirements the patch MUST satisfy. Each MUST be mappable to evidence at the done-gate.

must_not.description: Hard constraints the patch MUST NOT violate (e.g., must_not change public API, must_not upgrade deps).

non_goals.description: Explicitly out-of-scope items. Naming them prevents scope creep.

id.description: Stable AC identifier, e.g., AC1, AC2.

condition.description: Observable, machine-checkable condition whenever possible (test name, lint rule, build target, etc.).

acceptance_criteria.description: At least one acceptance criterion is required. The done-gate walks every AC.

allowed_scope.description: Files / directories / modules the patch is allowed to touch. Any change outside this list is a scope violation.

preserved_invariants.description: Invariants that must continue to hold after the patch (e.g., 'public function X signature unchanged', 'JSON config remains valid').

blocking_unknowns.description: Open questions that block implementation. If non-empty, the task is BLOCKED until resolved with the user.

risk_tier.description: Risk/complexity tier used by the dynamic router (see core/routing.md).
